How certain is the authorship of Zhuangzi (莊子)?

The authorship of the Zhuangzi (莊子) is classified as 'compiled,' which means scholars believe the text was assembled by multiple authors or editors rather than written entirely by a single person. While the philosopher Zhuangzi is the traditional namesake and likely contributed to the 'inner chapters,' the 'outer' and 'miscellaneous' chapters are generally thought to reflect the work of later disciples and commentators. This means we cannot be fully confident that any given passage traces directly back to Zhuangzi himself, making the attribution a matter of scholarly tradition rather than certain historical fact.
